## Module Overview

{{MODULE_OVERVIEW}}

<!--
Explain what this directory does in the context of the overall AIA Core system.
Include: what layer of the architecture this is (routes / data / schemas / services),
what domain concepts it covers, and how it connects to other modules.

Example (src/app/routes/):
This directory contains all FastAPI route handlers — the HTTP API surface of AIA Core.
Each file defines a router for one domain: game sessions, individual hands, players,
community card images, CSV uploads, stats, and search. Routers are registered in
`src/app/main.py` with their respective prefixes. All route handlers depend on
`src/app/database/` for persistence and validate input/output through
`src/pydantic_models/`.
-->

## Open Questions

{{OPEN_QUESTIONS}}

<!--
Module-level gaps — patterns or inconsistencies that span multiple artifacts in this directory.

Example (for src/app/routes/):
**Q1 — Inconsistent error handling across routers**
- **Observation:** Some routers raise `HTTPException(404)` on missing resources; others return `None` or an empty list. The `game.py` router silently returns `{}` for unknown game IDs.
- **Why it matters:** API consumers cannot reliably distinguish "not found" from "empty result" without a consistent 404 pattern.
- **Suggested resolution:** Standardize on `HTTPException(status_code=404, detail="<resource> not found")` across all routers. See individual route docs for specific instances.

Use "None identified." if no cross-cutting gaps were found.
-->
